# Daily Report — 2026-06-04

## Task Completed
Added `create_ci_outage` tool (POST cmdb_ci_outage). The tool opens a new outage
record for a CI with required fields `cmdb_ci` (affected CI sys_id) and `begin`
(outage start datetime). Optional fields: `type`, `end`, `short_description`,
`cause_ci`, `resolved` (serialised as "true"/"false"), and `resolution_notes`.
Datetime fields (`begin`, `end`) are validated by the existing
`validate_servicenow_datetime` helper. Registered in `system_administrator` and
`full` tool packages.

## Test Results
- Total tests: 1394
- Coverage: 92%
- All passing: yes

## Security
Pre-existing environment vulnerabilities in pyjwt, setuptools, urllib3, wheel
(not introduced by this change). No new vulnerabilities.

## Files Changed
- src/servicenow_mcp/tools/cmdb_tools.py — added CreateCIOutageParams + create_ci_outage
- src/servicenow_mcp/tools/__init__.py — exported create_ci_outage
- src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py — registered CreateCIOutageParams + create_ci_outage_tool
- config/tool_packages.yaml — added create_ci_outage to system_administrator and full packages
- tests/test_cmdb_ci_outage_tools.py — 13 new tests (params validation + function behaviour)

## Next Up
Backlog is now empty — time to add new items. Candidate: add update_ci_outage tool
(PATCH cmdb_ci_outage/{sys_id} to set end time, resolution notes, or mark resolved).
